stories.title: Czech MEP’s undeclared think-tank role raises conflict-of-interest questions
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Alexandr Vondra, a Czech member of the European Parliament, did not disclose his role as honorary chair of an influential think tank in the Czech Republic, despite negotiating files affecting the companies funding the organization. Transparency International argues that Vondra’s failure to disclose his relationship with the Prague Center of Transatlantic Relations may violate the European Parliament’s code of conduct. The issue was first flagged to POLITICO by Aria, a clim

stories.summary: Nigel Farage’s party published the donations as part of its requirements with the Electoral Commission At the start of the week, the Times reported that Darren Jones , the chief secretary to the Treasury, was thinking of standing to be the next Labour leader. The prospect of Jones replacing Keir Starmer always seemed a bit of a long shot. But today it is safe to say the Jones campaign is definitely over. stories.summary: El Dorado blaze that burned 22,744 acres and claimed the life of a firefighter was ignited by an illegal device Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email Nearly six years after a couple’s gender-reveal stunt sparked a deadly wildfire in southern California , the companies that sold the pyrotechnic device have agreed to a multimillion-dollar settlement. The Hubbard, Ohio-based Wholesale Fireworks Corp and its subsidiary American Fireworks Wholesale LLC have agreed to pay more than $4m, th

stories.title: Ukraine moves closer to EU accession after Hungary deal
stories.summary: Budapest will likely drop its veto on Kyiv joining the EU after reaching a deal on rights for Ukraine's ethnic Hungarian minority. But it remains opposed to Ukraine's accession being fast-tracked.

stories.title: Magyar announces agreement with Ukraine on rights of Hungarian minority
stories.summary: According to the Hungarian Prime Minister, Ukraine’s commitments will be reflected in the action plan agreed upon with the European Union.

Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva said he received the decision “with indignation”. The Brazil president also blamed the decision by the

stories.title: Egy hónappal a választás után szembejött a magyar focival a valóság
stories.summary: A közmédia eddig évi közel 10 milliárdot fizetett a magyar foci közvetítéséért, a jövőben ennél jóval kevesebb lehet a bevétel. A pályáztatási folyamattal most már igyekeznie kell az MLSZ-nek.

stories.title: Nem a pánik látszik abból, mit kezd a NER-elit a milliárdos profittal
stories.summary: Bár a számukra kedvezőtlen választási eredmény után nagy vagyonkimentésre lehetett számítani, az Orbán-korszak csúcsvállalatai a jövőre készülnek, legalábbis ezt lehet kiolvasni az éves jelentéseikből.

stories.summary: Bár a BYD körüli várakozások továbbra is magasan tartják az árakat, a szegedi lakáspiacon egyre több jel utal lassulásra.

stories.title: Magyar Péter a parlamentben akarja végleg megroppantani a Fideszt
stories.summary: Magyar Péter miniszterelnökként a kampányban látott kemény stílusban osztja ki a fideszes politikusokat a parlamentben. A beszólogatásokkal már most elejét akarja venni, hogy a választási vereségtől megrendült Fidesz később támadni tudja a kormányát, amikor előjönnek a kényesebb ügyek.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Egy olyan országot kell építenünk, ahol nemcsak mi szeretjük a hazánkat, hanem a hazánk is viszontszeret minket
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök a trianoni szerződés nemzeti emléknapján mondott beszédet a Kossuth téren.

stories.title: Új román törvény feszegeti Nyirő József és Wass Albert helyét az erdélyi emlékezetben
stories.summary: Az RMDSZ is támogatta a törvényt, amely alapján ismét előkerült Wass és Nyirő neve. Wassnak már eddig is legfeljebb csellel lehetett szobrot állítani Romániában, Nyirő nevét viszont utca és több iskola is viseli Erdélyben.

stories.title: Saját részvényeit veszi a tőzsdén a Magyar Telekom, így segíti a tulajdonosait
stories.summary: Ha egy cég a készpénzéből a tulajdonosainak szeretne juttatni, fizethet osztalékot (dividend) vagy belekezdhet egy részvényvisszavásárlási (buyback) programba.

stories.title: Aranyat ér az új ügyfél a bankoknak, akár 150 ezer forint is jár nekünk
stories.summary: Egyre feljebb srófolják a bankok azt az összeget, amennyit bezsebelhet tőlük egy új ügyfél a számlanyitásért. Az elmúlt hetekben újabb jóváírási akciók indultak, és a már meglévőket is vonzóbbá tették – derül ki a BiztosDöntés.hu által összegyűjtött adatokból. Az esetenként százezer forint feletti ajándékpénzekért cserébe viszont teljes elköteleződést várnak el a pénzintézetek.

stories.title: A magyar könyv ünnepe: négynapos fesztivállá alakul Budapest júniusban (x)
stories.summary: Június 11. és 14. között ismét a könyveké lesz a főszerep Budapest belvárosában: a Magyar Könyvkiadók és Könyvterjesztők Egyesülése megrendezi a 97. Ünnepi Könyvhetet. A Vörösmarty tér, a Vigadó tér és a Duna-korzó négy napon át a kortárs magyar irodalom, a könyvkiadás és a kulturális találkozások központjává válik, ahol mintegy kétszáz kiállító, több száz program, könyvbemutató, közel ezer dedikálás, koncert és beszélgetés várja az érdeklődőket.

stories.title: Meghalt Marjane Satrapi, a Persepolis rendezője
stories.summary: Az iráni-francia képregényes-filmrendező a saját gyerekkoráról szóló Persepolis rajzolójaként lett világhírű, 56 éves volt.

stories.title: Szijjártó alatt már meg sem lepődött senki, hogy nincs, Orbán Anita most 15 év után visszahozza
stories.summary: A legtöbb országban a külügyminisztériumok céljait és prioritásait részletesen kidolgozott stratégiákban szokás összefoglalni. 2011-ben még Orbán alatt is készült külpolitikai stratégia, mai szemmel meglepő alapvetésekkel. Szijjártó alatt hűlt helye volt, de a Magyar-kormány újból lefekteti, mik alapján képzeli el a nemzetközi kapcsolatokat és az ország helyét a világban.

The US’s attention is divided, limiting external pressure for compromise, while Moscow and Kyiv both still believe they can strengthen their respective negotiating positions through battlefield gains. At some point, however, a deal will have to be done. The parameters of that deal are already understood by negotiators on

stories.title: What the Hellenic! Why is Christopher Nolan’s new Greek epic entirely devoid of Greeks?
stories.summary: Set to be this year’s biggest blockbuster, The Odyssey’s cast has been selected to ‘represent the world’. Fair enough – except that one key country seems to have gone completely unrepresented … There are the American accents, gleaming body suits and a muddy Dunkirk palette. And then there is Lupita Nyong’o as Helen of Troy, a casting choice that recently drew racist attacks from the usual moaners of the internet , including Elon Musk, who complained it wasn’t authentic. stories.summary: Így még azelőtt lecsaphatnának a rendszer leendő ellenségeire, hogy bármit elkövettek volna, vagy akár csak megszülettek volna rendszerellenes gondolataik. Egy kínai cég már két éve dolgozik a rendszeren, ki tudja, meddig jutottak azóta. Így is csak idő kérdése, hogy valóssággá váljon a disztópikus sci-fik világa.

The Institute for the Study of War (ISW) thinktank said the strikes were also aimed at distracting from the impact of Ukrainian long-range attacks into Russia . stories.title: Labour is fighting two different campaigns in Makerfield

In the tight June 18 fight for the Greater Manchester seat, Burnham — who is making a dual pitch to be the local MP as well as a potential replacement for Labour’s own prime minister Keir Starmer — has been largely making an upbeat case to voters and insisting he is “just gonna talk about what I’m going to do.” That’s in stark contrast t

stories.title: Trump’s rebuilding his tariffs. They could end up a lot narrower.
stories.summary: President Donald Trump is slowly unveiling his new tariff regime to replace the one knocked down by the Supreme Court earlier this year. So far, it’s more restrained than the previous version — a nod to the country’s current economic realities. The Trump administration this week proposed new duties on more than 60 countries after hurried trade investigations — in many cases suggesting tariffs substantially lower than the illegal ones it imposed last year. stories.title: Trump expected to nominate Todd Blanche to be US attorney general
stories.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump is expected to nominate Todd Blanche to be attorney general, the position he’s held on an acting basis since April, administration officials said Wednesday. The president could nominate Blanche as soon as Wednesday night, two officials, who were granted anonymity to discuss private conversations, told POLITICO. stories.title: From approval to access: Europe’s next health imperative
stories.summary: Europe’s health ambition is returning to the political agenda. With a focus on clinical trials, biotechnology and cardiovascular health, the Health Package signals Brussels’ intent to prioritize innovation, research and prevention as pillars of Europe’s competitiveness and resilience.   But for many patients, one reality remains unchanged: access to innovative medicines remains too slow.  Today, European patients are waiting longer than ever to access

stories.title: Seattle, home to Amazon and Microsoft, poised to pass moratorium on new datacenters
stories.summary: Measure expected to succeed next week represents major rebuke to big tech as local disquiet over AI boom grows Seattle’s city government is on the verge of passing a year-long ban on the construction of new datacenters, the largest city yet in the US to consider such a moratorium as nationwide backlash grows. Four companies sought to build five large datacenters in areas serviced by Seattle’s public utility; if approved, they would have consumed approximately a third of the city’s current daily

stories.title: Tech industry wins big in California primary election with millions spent paying off
stories.summary: Silicon Valley is fighting against regulation, taxes and growth of AI and will benefit from having political leverage Silicon Valley had a big night in California’s primary election, proving that the tens of millions of dollars funding candidates across the state was money well spent. While the tech industry’s preferred candidate for governor came in a scant sixth place, donations to smaller elections proved to be a successful strategy. stories.title: NHS to curb political symbols on uniforms after antisemitism report
stories.summary: Government-ordered review reveals ‘routine ostracism’ of Jewish staff and patients in health service The NHS is taking action to tackle antisemitism after a government-ordered report found that Jewish patients and staff face “routine ostracism” in the service. Anti-Jewish hatred in the NHS means some patients hide their identity and staff “suffer in silence”, a review by Lord Mann, the government’s adviser on antisemitism, has found. Continue reading...

stories.title: Dead but deportable: US immigration judge signed order to eject teen murder victim
stories.summary: North Carolina judge said Levi Mendez-Maldonado failed to show up in court – even after being told he had died in 2024 An immigration judge in Charlotte, North Carolina , recently ordered the deportation of a young man who was killed in 2024, citing his failure to appear in court. Judge Amy Lee ordered the removal of Levi Mendez-Maldonado in absentia on 21 May. Mendez-Maldonado, originally from Honduras, came to the United States as an unaccompanied minor at age 17 and was murdered in a shooting

stories.title: The Great British break: A hidden regional growth engine
stories.summary: Walk through the center of most towns in England on a slow weekday afternoon and the story tells itself. The empty shopfront. The pub that didn’t reopen after the pandemic. The independent restaurant that could no longer afford staff. Return on the recent May bank holiday, the hottest on record, and the economic impact is visible in real time: buzzing beer gardens, busy cafés and thriving local attractions all benefiting from a surge in domestic travel as the sun makes an appearance. This

stories.title: EU Parliament vice president defects from center left to liberals
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — European Parliament Vice President Pina Picierno on Thursday left the Italian Democratic Party and the center-left group in the European Parliament, and is poised to join the liberals. Picierno, who has been an MEP since 2014, told the Italian newspaper Il Foglio that she was leaving the center-left party because some of its members were ambiguous in their support for Ukraine and in their condemnation of Russian President Vladimir Putin’s war against Kyiv. She added that the par

stories.title: Japan sees shortage of plastic bags, trays and gloves, as Iran war-induced naphtha shortage worsens
stories.summary: The Middle East is Japan’s main source of crude oil, from which naphtha is extracted and used to make items including printing ink and plastics Takeaways, supermarkets, and bakeries in Japan are running out of plastic bags, trays and food service gloves amid widening shortages of the key plastic ingredient, naphtha, due to the Middle East crisis. The food sector accounts for nearly one-third of Japan’s annual plastic use of more than 8m tonnes, and price rises and shortages are hitting hard acro

stories.title: US House passes war powers resolution to curb Trump’s authority in Iran
stories.summary: Stunning rebuke to president as lawmakers vote 215-208 for measure forcing him to seek congressional approval The US House of Representatives delivered a stunning rebuke to Donald Trump over his war on Iran on Wednesday, as representatives backed a move to force him to seek approval from Congress or withdraw US forces. stories.title: Karen Bass heads to LA mayoral runoff but challenger yet to be determined
stories.summary: Incumbent leads in primary, but with less than 50% of votes must face either Spencer Pratt or Nithya Raman Los Angeles’s high-profile mayoral contest remained unsettled Wednesday evening as the city waited to learn who will join incumbent Karen Bass in November’s general election. Bass came out ahead in Tuesday’s heated primary but, after securing less than 50% of the vote, she’ll have to defend her seat against either Spencer Pratt, a former reality TV star, or progressive city council member N

stories.title: Oman resists US pressure to break ties with Iran over strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Sultanate says talks with Tehran are limited to lawful management of waterway, but Washington has doubts about neutrality Oman is resisting US pressure to break its links with Iran, and insists it has only been negotiating with Tehran on a future management system for the strait of Hormuz that would be compliant with international law. The aim would be to implement any regime after consulting the UN’s International Maritime Organization (IMO). Traditionally Oman, a longtime US ally that shares s

stories.title: Rebel attacks in eastern DRC kill 30 people and hamper Ebola response
stories.summary: Islamic State-linked militia blamed for raids in North Kivu as governor says three patients with disease fled clinics Rebel attacks around a town that is one of the centres of the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo have left more than 30 people dead over the past few days, complicating the response to the disease. At least 10 people were massacred in raids on three villages around the city of Beni, in North Kivu, in the early hours of Wednesday morning. Continue reading...

stories.title: A good life for the 99% isn’t a pipe dream: it can be done. Here’s how
stories.summary: Our plan is radical – but by transforming how we live on a finite planet, nearly everyone gains ‘An equal and habitable world is possible’: academics set out sweeping vision for planetary survival Imagine a future in which everyone enjoys high levels of wellbeing; where 90% of the world’s population doubles their income but works half the hours we work today. A world in which the bottom half of humanity sees its share of global wealth rise from just 2% today to 30%; a world where we consume enou

stories.title: Edinburgh festivals hope to launch joint box office for all 11 events
stories.summary: Bosses believe single booking process will drive up ticket sales for all festivals to offset funding squeeze The Edinburgh festivals hope to launch a single box office for all the city’s 11 festivals to make it simpler to buy tickets and profit from the “lake” of customer data they hold. Festival directors hope a universal box office will allow them to increase ticket sales and attract a wealthy corporate sponsor, such as Mastercard, to offset deep cuts in public funding they expect to see in co

stories.title: Australian woman linked to Islamic State lived with teenage slave who was repeatedly raped, court told
stories.summary: Allegations made in Melbourne magistrates court on Thursday as Zeinab Ahmad, 31, applied for bail, a month after she was charged with slavery offences Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A woman accused of marrying Islamic State fighters allegedly lived with a teenage slave who was repeatedly assaulted and raped by the woman’s father. The allegations were detailed in Melbourne magistrates court on Thursday as Zeinab Ahmad, 31, applied for bail, a month after she was char

stories.title: Two men charged after Southampton disorder over Henry Nowak murder
stories.summary: Matt Styler charged with assaulting officer and Daniel Frost with violent disorder and possession of offensive weapon Two men have been charged in connection with the disorder that broke out in Southampton after the sentencing of a man for the murder of 18-year-old Henry Nowak. Eleven police officers were injured in the disturbance, which took place close to the home of the killer, Vickrum Digwa. Continue reading...

stories.title: UK university’s axing of black studies MA has ‘dangerous parallel’ with US, says academic
stories.summary: Civil rights scholar Kimberlé Crenshaw warns Birmingham City University’s decision part of extremist campaign that has ‘travelled across Atlantic’ A leading US civil rights scholar has urged Birmingham City University (BCU) to reverse its decision to close its black studies course, comparing it with the attack on diversity, equity and inclusion in the US. Kimberlé Crenshaw, a professor of law at the University of California, Los Angeles and Columbia University, expressed “profound concern” about

stories.title: Kidnappings, threats and ‘protection fees’: how can Mexico confront rise in deadly extortion?
stories.summary: With corrupt police on the streets and shopkeepers forced to pay gangs, president has vowed to tackle crime that now affects all parts of society It was about 11pm and Luis* was about to get into an Uber to go home when the police car pulled up. One of the officers frisked him and produced two plastic bags with what looked like drugs: one contained some sort of powder, the other little crystals. Luis had never seen them before. Luis, who asked not to use his real name for fear of reprisals, insi

stories.title: EU aims to ensure foreign governments or firms cannot disrupt tech services with ‘kill switch’
stories.summary: European Commission proposals aim to reduce ‘risky dependencies’ on foreign suppliers in cloud computing, AI and semiconductors The EU executive wants to ensure no foreign government or company has access to a “kill switch” to turn off or disrupt vital tech services across the continent, as part of an effort to cut dependencies on the US and China. Publishing “technological sovereignty” proposals that risk further tensions with Donald Trump, the European Commission said on Wednesday the bloc nee

stories.title: Moment explosion erupts at Russian oil terminal in St. Petersburg after Ukrainian drone attack
stories.summary: Black smoke billowed over St. Petersburg after a Ukrainian drone strike sparked a fire at an oil terminal, according to Ukrainian and Russian officials. The attack came days before President Vladimir Putin is due to attend a major economic forum in the city.

stories.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX aiming to raise $75bn in record IPO
stories.summary: If completed, the unprecedented IPO would surpass the record $25.6 billion raised by Saudi oil producer Aramco in 2019 and could make Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire.

stories.title: Israel, Lebanon renew ceasefire after US-brokered talks
stories.summary: The agreement comes as hostilities between Israel and Lebanese militants Hezbollah have intensified in recent weeks. It is unclear if the renewed truce will affect stalled talks between the US and Iran. DW has more.

stories.title: North Korea unveils nuclear weapons fuel production plant
stories.summary: Kim Jong Un called for North Korea's capacity to produce weapons-grade uranium to grow at an "exponential rate."

stories.title: Germany sees record number of discrimination claims in 2025
stories.summary: The federal commissioner for anti-discrimination has said the government's reform plans, the first in 20 years, are inadequate and won't provide the resources and funding needed to combat discrimination.

stories.title: Tanzania's president visits Russia amid frayed Western ties
stories.summary: President Samia Suluhu Hassan is on a three-day visit to Russia, traveling with a business delegation. Relations with Western nations have cratered in recent months amid criticism of Tanzania's handling of dissent.

stories.title: Erin Brockovich: Environmentalist against the data center boom
stories.summary: Do you know where a data center is due to be constructed in your area? An increasing number of US citizens can answer "yes" — thanks to a map created by Erin Brockovich. DW explains why people need to know.

stories.title: India news: Delhi orders fire safety crackdown after deadly hotel fire
stories.summary: Hours after a fire at a New Delhi hotel killed 21 people, the establishment's owner has been arrested. Delhi is also launching a fire safety inspection. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Iran war: Even a peace deal won't fix energy crunch
stories.summary: The growing energy and supply chain crisis is unlikely to ease even if the US and Iran step back from the brink. Experts warn that reopening the Strait of Hormuz and repairing infrastructure could take months or years.

stories.title: China bans 4 New Zealand lawmakers after their Taiwan trip
stories.summary: Beijing says it is barring a group of New Zealand lawmakers from entering China for having visited Taiwan in May. It is the first time such a ban has been imposed on politicians from New Zealand.

stories.title: 2026 World Cup: 6 things you need to know
stories.summary: The 2026 World Cup is to be bigger than all of its predecessors. Whether it will be better is an entirely different question. DW fills you in on what you need to know.

stories.title: $850 million Obama Presidential Center prepares for opening
stories.summary: The museum dedicated to former US Presdient Barack Obama is set to open on June 19. Spanning 19.3 acres, the campus will house a museum, library, and park.

stories.title: 100 days of Iran war: Gulf states grapple with security and economic consequences
stories.summary: The US-Israel war with Iran has forced the Gulf states to reassess long-held assumptions about security, alliances and stability in their region. What does this mean for the future?

stories.title: Germany loses vote for UN Security Council seat
stories.summary: Fifteen of the 193 UN member states sit on the UN Security Council. Germany was in competition with Austria and Portugal for two seats in the "Western Europe and Others" group.

stories.title: Grab what you can while you can: The new reality in the South China Sea
stories.summary: After years of watching China create land to back its expansive claims, others are doing the same.

stories.title: Costly fuel pushes more Indians to buy electric cars but challenges remain
stories.summary: High fuel prices are strengthening the case for EV adoption in the world's third largest auto market.

stories.title: Why Canada has generic Ozempic, and the US doesn't
stories.summary: Canada is the first G7 country to approve the lower cost weight-loss drugs, which are rolling out in pharmacies this month.

stories.title: Germany blames Russia for 'bitter defeat' in UN Security Council bid
stories.summary: Johann Wadephul said it was "no secret" Russia had stirred up sentiment against Germany over its support for Ukraine.

stories.title: Israel and Lebanon agree to conditional ceasefire following US-led talks
stories.summary: The ceasefire comes as Israel has recently escalated its attacks and is staging its deepest ground offensive into Lebanon in two decades.

stories.title: China bans four New Zealand MPs over Taiwan visit
stories.summary: Upon their return from Taiwan last month, the lawmakers were told that China had banned them for a year.

stories.title: Black Hawks and robodogs - how one Mexican city is preparing for the World Cup
stories.summary: BBC’s Will Grant got a first-hand look at security preparations in Monterrey, as the city gets ready to host four matches.

stories.title: Cuba’s fuel crisis leaves Havana’s streets overflowing with rubbish
stories.summary: Cuba is facing a mounting waste management crisis as fuel shortages continue to disrupt rubbish collection across Havana, the country's capital.

stories.title: US House delivers rebuke to Trump as it votes to halt Iran war
stories.summary: In the 215-208 vote, four Republicans joined Democrats to pass the measure, which is largely symbolic.

stories.title: Israel and Lebanon agree to implement ceasefire if Hezbollah stops attacks
stories.summary: The countries reject "any attempt, by any state or non-state actor, to hold Lebanon's future hostage", the US says.

stories.title: France: Protesters rally outside CNews TV over alleged Kremlin influence
stories.summary: France witnessed renewed debate over foreign influence in the media on 3 June as dozens of protesters gathered outside CNews headquarters in Paris to denounce Ksenia Fedorova, the former head of RT France.

stories.title: Trump to nominate Blanche for attorney general on permanent basis
stories.summary: If confirmed by the Senate, Trump's former personal lawyer would become the administration's number one prosecutor.

stories.title: Uneasy neighbours: Can three World Cup hosts put differences aside for a month?
stories.summary: The tournament comes after a period of fractious relations between the US, Canada and Mexico.

stories.title: Ukraine accused of killing four in occupied Crimea
stories.summary: At least 10 people have been injured in the attacks, according to Russian-backed officials.

stories.title: Drone attack on UAE's Barakah more dangerous than Zaporizhzhia, IAEA chief tells Euronews
stories.summary: Rafael Grossi told Euronews the drone strike on the operating UAE nuclear plant posed greater risks than Zaporizhzhia because its reactors were live at the time of the attack, warning that tonnes of nuclear material could have triggered a radiological accident with "very, very serious consequences".

stories.title: Ukrainian drones hit St Petersburg as Putin's flagship economic forum opens
stories.summary: Several long-range drones struck oil storage facilities near the city, days before Vladimir Putin is due to address the event.

stories.title: At least 21 people, mostly foreign nationals, killed in New Delhi hotel fire, police say
stories.summary: Several of those killed were foreigners, mainly from Central Asia and Africa, the Press Trust of India news agency reported, quoting unnamed officials.

stories.title: US announces new tariffs over forced labour concerns
stories.summary: It comes after the US Supreme Court struck down many of US President Donald Trump's previous duties in February.

stories.title: 'Crazy' phone call between Trump and Netanyahu complicates Iran talks
stories.summary: Israel's PM laughed off reports of friction, but he has tested the patience of other US presidents.

stories.title: Hundreds of Russian strikes across Ukraine kill at least 14 people – video
stories.summary: Russia launched 73 missiles and 656 drones at Ukraine overnight, according to the Ukrainian air force, including eight hypersonic Tsirkon missiles. The main targets were Kyiv, Dnipro, Zaporizhzhia, Poltava and Kharkiv. The Ukrainian president, Volodymyr Zelenskyy, has asked his US counterpart, Donald Trump, to send Patriot missiles to the country after the Russian attacks killed at least 14 people and injured dozens more Zelenskyy asks Trump to send missiles after Russian strikes across Ukraine

stories.title: Portugal: General strike causes disruption to services
stories.summary: Flights have been canceled and schools closed as unions stage a strike over government labor reform plans. It is the second major walkout in Portugal in six months.

stories.title: Republicans press Trump over controversial €1.5bn fund for victims of 'political weaponisation'
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump's Anti-Weaponisation Fund, launched to compensate individuals who believe they were subjected to politically motivated investigations, is being questioned by senators in his own party.

stories.title: Iranian drone attack causes heavy damage to Kuwait airport as US and Iran trade strikes
stories.summary: Kuwait suspended commercial flights after drones heavily damaged its international airport, hours after Iran fired missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain and the US struck an Iranian military facility on Qeshm Island. Iran's IRGC said it had targeted the US Navy's 5th Fleet headquarters in Bahrain.

stories.title: ‘It’s a relief … I’m irrelevant!’: Rufus Norris on life after the National Theatre
stories.summary: He stood down as boss of the NT – and threw himself into kayaking, writing and DIY. The veteran director talks about his new start aged 60, mourning his mother – and directing Death of a Salesman in Turkish There were several big endings for Rufus Norris in 2025, all crammed into the same few seismic months. Firstly, the close of his tenure as director of the National Theatre after a decade at the helm. That planned ending collided with the loss of his mother, who died three weeks before he left

stories.title: ​Why is Europe still not ready for extreme heat?
stories.summary: ​The first heatwaves of the season reveal how ​ill-prepared governments across the continent are to protect people from increasingly dangerous temperatures • Don’t get This Is Europe delivered to your inbox? Sign up here Meteorological summer has begun, ushered in with scorching heat that struck before spring was up. Although western Europe is now mostly free from last week’s heat dome – which shattered temperature records for May in the UK and Ireland – it is already bracing for yet another swe

stories.title: DRC Ebola outbreak could have begun as early as January, WHO chief says
stories.summary: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus says the virus ‘had a big head start’ but that the response was catching up The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo could have begun as early as January, the head of the World Health Organization said, giving the virus “a big head start”. Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus also said the response was being hindered by blanket travel restrictions and highlighted high levels of community mistrust and low levels of contact tracing as key concerns. Continue re

stories.title: Wim Wenders withdraws 1975 film featuring 13-year-old Nastassja Kinski topless
stories.summary: German director says he recognises actor should have been better protected during filming of Wrong Move German director Wim Wenders has withdrawn from circulation his 1975 film Wrong Move, because of a scene featuring a child actor topless who was 13 at the time of filming. The director said in a statement released on Wednesday: “Streaming, TV and distribution partners have been instructed to no longer make the film publicly accessible.” Continue reading...

stories.title: Saudi Arabia and Russia draw closer amid OPEC upheaval
stories.summary: As Saudi Arabia and Russia deepen cooperation, their combined influence over global oil supply is growing. Could this shift the balance of power in energy markets?

stories.title: Google mosquito army: Scientists say 'we must take action'
stories.summary: Google's Debug research program plans to release millions of sterile mosquitoes to fight species that spread diseases like dengue. How does the method work — and should humans interfere with nature like this?

stories.title: Wim Wenders withdraws film with disputed child nude scene
stories.summary: Actress Nastassja Kinski spent years calling for the removal of a sexualized scene from Wim Wenders' film "The Wrong Move," in which she appeared topless at the age of 13.

stories.title: Taliban, Russia are cozying up to each other — why?
stories.summary: Russia and the Afghan Taliban have signed a deal on repairing Soviet-era and Russian-made weapons, a seemingly small move in a larger game involving the US, Pakistan and Moscow's efforts to curb Western influence.

stories.title: German hotel apologizes for 'no Jews allowed' message
stories.summary: A Bavarian hotel has apologized after rejecting a booking by an Israeli customer by telling them that "there are no Jews allowed" at the hotel.

stories.title: UK Royal Navy helicopter crash leaves 3 dead
stories.summary: Prime Minister Keir Starmer said the deaths were "utterly tragic," while the cause of the accident remains unknown.

stories.title: Ebola had 'big head-start,' WHO chief Tedros warns
stories.summary: World Health Organization chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus had recently visited the epicenter of the outbreak in the DR Congo. He said the "virus is ahead of us, we need to move faster."

stories.title: Foreign nationals among at least 21 killed in Delhi fire
stories.summary: Many victims were South Asians who had travelled to India for treatment or to accompany relatives.

stories.title: Fire tears through New Delhi building, killing at least 18
stories.summary: A fire in a New Delhi building killed at least 18 people and injured several others, with rescue teams pulling dozens from the Malviya Nagar block.

stories.title: Trump confirms calling Netanyahu 'f****** crazy' in telephone row over Lebanon strikes
stories.summary: The comments provide a rare public glimpse into tensions between the two leaders, who have long presented a united front despite occasional disagreements behind closed doors.

stories.title: SOCAR, TotalEnergies and ADNOC lock in Caspian gas supply deal
stories.summary: Azerbaijan's SOCAR signed a long-term deal with TotalEnergies, ADNOC's investment arm XRG and Turkey's BOTAŞ to export gas from the Absheron offshore field from 2029, bolstering the Southern Gas Corridor that Europe has increasingly relied on in recent years.

stories.title: Director Wim Wenders withdraws 1975 film over actress's teen topless scene
stories.summary: Nastassja Kinski, who appeared in the film when she was 13, said the film-maker "didn't protect me".

stories.title: Ex-UK political aide and Biden cabinet secretary lead California governor primary vote count
stories.summary: Another Democrat, billionaire climate activist Tom Steyer, is in third place as the tally continues.

stories.title: FBI agents fatally shoot man holding hostages in California building, police say
stories.summary: Nearby buildings, including City Hall and the police headquarters, were evacuated and some roads were temporarily closed during the hostage situation.

stories.title: Four sentenced to death for killing worshippers at Catholic church in Nigeria
stories.summary: A fifth man was discharged and acquitted by the court due to insufficient evidence against him.

stories.title: The end of Trump's 'anti-weaponisation' fund is another sign Republicans are fighting back
stories.summary: The $1.8bn fund would have paid people the Trump administration decided were unjustly and politically targeted.

stories.title: From butterflies to breast milk, Uber's list of lost items reveals wild backseat discoveries
stories.summary: The rideshare company's annual Lost & Found Index also reveals America's most forgetful city, and the most forgetful day.

stories.title: Kyrgyzstan elected to UN Security Council for first time in its history
stories.summary: Kyrgyzstan was elected to the UN Security Council as a non-permanent member for 2027-2028, marking a rare win for Central Asia.

stories.title: A válogatott focista, akit tőrbe csaltak, és 29 évesen felakasztottak Rákosiék
stories.summary: 75 éve ezen a napon ért véget az újpesti középhátvéd, Szűcs Sándor élete. Szerelmével, Kovács Erzsi énekesnővel szerettek volna disszidálni, de az ÁVH mozgatta a szálakat. A kommunizmus egyik legsötétebb ügye.

stories.title: Újabb hat helyen találtak azbesztet a zúzottkőben Zalaegerszegen
stories.summary: Pénteken két helyszínről eltávolítják az azbesztes kőzúzalékot, a közeli bölcsődék, óvodák, iskolák arra kérik majd a szülőket, aznap hamar vigyék el a gyerekeket.

stories.title: Izrael és Libanon megállapodott a tűzszünet végrehajtásáról
stories.summary: Az újabb bejelentést még nem kommentálta a Hezbollah.

stories.title: Visszatér a napos idő, hogy aztán pénteken megint itt hagyjon minket

stories.summary: A cég szerint még nem született végleges döntés a munkavállalók elbocsátásáról. A döntés több mint 180 dolgozót érinthet.

stories.title: Tőzsdére lép a SpaceX: ez minden idők legnagyobb ilyen tranzakciója lesz
stories.summary: Tőzsdére lép a SpaceX, ezzel Elon Musk vagyona azonnal átlépheti az 1000 milliárd dollár. Óriási a lelkesedés, de azért a befektetőknek vállalniuk kell a Musk-rizikót.

stories.title: Vitézyvel egyeztetne a reklámszövetség a gyűlöletkeltő politikai plakátok visszaszorításáról szóló javaslatról
stories.summary: Sajnálják, hogy a piac érintettjeivel nem egyeztetett a kormány a javaslat előkészítésekor.

stories.title: Az áprilisi kiskereskedelmi forgalom súlyos rendszerhibákra figyelmeztet
stories.summary: A tavalyi bázishoz képest nőtt a forgalom, de a választási költési őrület alábbhagyott. Ugyanakkor továbbra is az üzemanyagpiaci fogyasztásbővülés a legerősebb, ami jól jelzi a védett ár esztelenségét.

stories.title: Két fideszes politikus letartóztatását kezdeményezte az ügyészség az óbudai korrupciós ügyben
stories.summary: Puskás Péter volt óbudai alpolgármester és Láng Zsolt volt második kerületi polgármester is előzetesbe kerülhet, miközben egy harmadik fideszes politikus, Gór Csaba lemondott a PADME Alapítvány vezetéséről.

stories.title: Újabb politikusokat gyanúsítottak meg az óbudai korrupciós ügyben, nyolc gyanúsított letartóztatását indítványozza az ügyészség
stories.summary: Az ügyészség szerint 2 milliárd forintnál is több vesztegetési pénzt fizettek ki az ügyben.

stories.title: Medvét láttak Pásztó közelében
stories.summary: Június 3-án, délelőtt észlelték az állatot a várostól délre. Korábban az Aggteleki Nemzeti Park közelében láttak barnamedvét.

stories.summary: A nyolc gyanúsított letartóztatásáról csütörtökön dönt a bíróság.

stories.summary: A Karakter e heti programajánlójában szerepel könyvbemutató, kiállításmegnyitó, és Booker-díjas szerző is.

stories.title: Ejtette az ügyészség a Pécs Pride szervezője ellen emelt vádat
stories.summary: Az Európai Bíróság ítéletére hivatkozva, amely szerint a gyermekvédelmi törvény uniós jogokat sértett.

stories.title: Dohányellenes sikerből maffiapiac? Az ausztrál példa sötét oldala
stories.summary: Ausztrália évtizedeken át a hagyományos dohányzás visszaszorításának egyik kiemelkedő nemzetközi példája volt. Elsőként vezetett be egységes, márkajelzés nélküli cigarettásdobozokat, eltüntette a dohányreklámokat, erős képi figyelmeztetéseket tett a csomagolásra, kitiltotta a dohányzást a legtöbb nyilvános térből, és folyamatosan emelte a legális dohánytermékek jövedéki adóját. A cél világos volt: legyen drága, kényelmetlen, társadalmilag egyre kevésbé elfogadott szokás.

stories.title: Karácsony Gergely ellen is ejtették a Pride szervezése miatt emelt vádat
stories.summary: Az Európai Bíróság áprilisi ítélete szerint uniós jogot sértett a gyermekvédelmi törvény, amelyre a Pride betiltását alapozták.

stories.title: A MOK javaslatot készít a családi összeférhetetlenség kezelésére az egészségügyben
stories.summary: A MOK szerint ezeket engedélyeztetni kellene a minisztériummal.

stories.title: Rosamund Pike kiakadt egy telefonozó néző miatt a West Enden
stories.summary: A színésznő meghajlás után még a színpadról szólt be a nézőnek, azt mondta, reméli, hogy a telefonon küldött üzenet „nagyon fontos” volt.

stories.title: One-ton cocaine bust reveals secret US-Mexico tunnel
stories.summary: Members of Jalisco New Generation cartel used fake retail store in San Diego as front for trafficking drugs, officials say Federal prosecutors have charged four suspects with trafficking more than one ton of cocaine for the Jalisco New Generation cartel using a fake retail store in San Diego as a front for a sophisticated tunnel that ran across the border to Tijuana, Mexico. The defendants include two Mexican nationals and two Americans charged with conspiring to traffic drugs across the US-Mexi

stories.title: UK: Protest erupts over student murder
stories.summary: Protesters have clashed with police in Southampton over the December murder of finance student Henry Nowak. The fatally injured victim was handcuffed by police after his killer accused him of racist abuse.

stories.title: Moment bear chases and attacks workers in Japan
stories.summary: CCTV footage captured a bear chasing and attacking a worker outside a factory in Fukushima. Four people were injured as the animal wandered through a residential area, prompting a large-scale response by police and emergency services.

stories.title: Two people shot dead in Kenya protests against Ebola plan
stories.summary: Kenyan police shot and killed two people who were protesting a US-backed plan to establish an Ebola quarantine facility in central Kenya.

stories.title: Italy marks 80 years of the Republic with military parade in Rome
stories.summary: Italy celebrated the 80th anniversary of the birth of the Republic with ceremonies, military parades and flypasts in Rome, commemorating the 1946 vote that paved the way for democracy.

stories.title: EU Commission set to slam Italy's fuel duty cuts as energy prices spike
stories.summary: The EU Commission is set to criticise the Italian government's reduction in excise duties on fuels in a report to be published on Wednesday. Rome has been asking for more fiscal flexibility to address the energy crisis, which Brussels deems should be directed at vulnerable families and industries.

stories.title: ‘You’re f****** crazy’: Trump clashes with Netanyahu as Lebanon tensions rise
stories.summary: Trump rebukes Benjamin Netanyahu over Lebanon escalation, warning strikes risk isolating Israel and derailing Iran talks, according to reports

stories.title: US and Iran exchange new strikes as ceasefire talks falter over Israel's military actions in Lebanon
stories.summary: Iran fired missiles toward Kuwait and Bahrain but failed to hit their targets, with two of those fired at Kuwait falling apart en route, while those aimed at Bahrain were intercepted, the US Central Command said.

stories.title: UK: Police and protesters clash at Southampton rally over student killing
stories.summary: Clashes broke out between protesters and police in Southampton after the release of bodycam footage linked to the fatal stabbing of student Henry Nowak sparked public anger.

stories.title: 11 officers injured in Henry Nowak murder protests in the UK, police say
stories.summary: In a joint statement, a conglomerate of Sikh community groups condemned what it called a "moment of madness by an individual".

stories.title: Mozambique says five citizens killed in ‘xenophobic attacks’ in South Africa
stories.summary: South African police confirm two deaths of Mozambicans in Mossel Bay as anti-immigration violence sweeps country Mozambique said five of its nationals were killed in “xenophobic attacks” in South Africa at the weekend and efforts were under way on Tuesday to repatriate hundreds of others. However, the South African police confirmed only that two Mozambicans had died in violence in the southern coastal town of Mossel Bay, the first killings to be officially linked to a wave of anti-migrant protes

stories.title: UK wins court case over canceled plan to deport asylum seekers to Rwanda
stories.summary: An international court has rejected a Rwandan claim for millions in damages over a scrapped deal to send asylum seekers to the East African nation.

stories.title: ‘We don’t have another country to run to’: Kenyans fear US plan for Ebola quarantine site
stories.summary: People from town of potential site for US citizens exposed to Ebola say it puts them at risk in country with no known cases People from a town in central Kenya where the US wants to set up an Ebola quarantine facility for its citizens have strongly criticised the plan, saying they fear it will expose them to the virus and that it is indicative of double standards on the part of the US. “Everybody should be quarantined in their home country. We shouldn’t allow foreigners to bring us diseases,” sa

stories.title: Trump egy interjúban elismerte, hogy kibaszott őrültnek nevezte Netanjahut
stories.summary: Saját elmondása szerint nem volt dühös az izraeli miniszterelnökre, csak felkavarta, hogy folyton összetűzésbe keveredik Libanonnal.

stories.title: A Keresztapába illő leszámolás vezetett a szerb főrendőr bukásához, ami miatt most Vučić feje is főhet
stories.summary: Éttermi meghívás, becsempészett fegyver, korrupt rendőrség – kész lenne a recept egy jó filmhez, de ez a szerb valóság. Egy alvilági leszámolást a belgrádi rendőrfőkapitány személyesen próbált meg eltussolni, a botrány pedig a szerb kormánynak is kellemetlen.

stories.title: Egy medvévé változó lakótelepi kamasz története és egy amazon törzs beavatási szertartása nyerte az idei Friss Húst
stories.summary: A díjazással Konrád Zsuzsanna Valami vad című filmje és Kreif Zsuzsanna ADGWA-ATA című alkotása automatikusan felkerül arra a listára, ahonnan kiválasztják az Oscar-díjra jelölt filmeket.

stories.title: Pintér Bence kiegyezik azzal, hogy csökken a fizetése, ha ez az ára annak, hogy Győr visszakapja az elvont forrásokat
stories.summary: A győri polgármester a Szinten túl adásában arról is beszélt, támogatja, hogy valaki csak két cikluson keresztül legyen miniszterelnök, de a polgármesterek esetében ezt nem tartja jó ötletnek.

stories.title: Ápolók bántalmazhattak egy mozgáskorlátozott beteget a Jahn Ferenc Dél-pesti Kórházban, a rendőrség nyomoz
stories.summary: Az 55 éves nő szerint az ápolók öltöztetés közben odavágták az ágyhoz, ököllel ütötték a vállát és a hátát.

stories.title: Enyhíteni próbál az EU a digitális kiszolgáltatottságán
stories.summary: Csipgyártás, mesterséges intelligencia, felhőszolgáltatás, nyílt forráskódú programok, digitalizált energiaellátás is szerepel a digitális önrendelkezési csomagban, ami máris kapott kritikákat.

stories.title: Gajdos László segítségét kéri a gödi polgármester a Samsung-gyár zajszennyezése miatt
stories.summary: Kammerer Zoltán szerint a város nem tud mit tenni az akkugyár zajkibocsátása ellen, a kormányhivataltól választ sem kaptak.

stories.title: Legendary warrior women: The history of the Amazons
stories.summary: They're said to have cut off their breasts to better draw their bows and even to have killed their male offspring. What is the history behind the myths surrounding the Amazons?

stories.title: Unpacking Germany's campaign for a UN Security Council seat
stories.summary: On Wednesday, the United Nations will hold elections for several seats on the UN Security Council. Germany's government is campaigning vigorously for a seat.

stories.title: Kosovo general election: the cost of political polarization
stories.summary: Sunday's election in Kosovo — the third in under 16 months — is marked by a clash between former allies PM Albin Kurti and ex-President Vjosa Osmani, deadlock in the dialogue with Serbia and obstacles to EU integration.

stories.title: Germany: Cologne Cathedral to charge tourists €12 for entry
stories.summary: Cologne Cathedral has laid out plans to charge tourist visitors, starting in July. They will be asked to pay €12 each, to help cover maintenance costs. It still plans to keep a small free area available for worshippers.

stories.title: Armenia's election offers a choice between Russia and EU
stories.summary: Armenians are set to vote on the country's future geopolitical direction on June 7. The election will be a test of public opinion in the long-standing Russia ally, where the population is increasingly eyeing the EU.

stories.title: Who owns the billions in Germany's dormant bank accounts?
stories.summary: Billions of euros sit untouched in dormant bank accounts in Germany. With heirs unaware and no central register in place, banks and politicians are debating who should ultimately control the money.

stories.title: Bear injures four in residential area of Japan as annual number of attacks rises
stories.summary: Japan's Environment Ministry said 13 people were killed in more than 230 bear attacks in 2025, exceeding the number of fatalities and attacks in any preceding year.

stories.title: Giant Lionel Messi statue dismantled in Kolkata
stories.summary: A 21-metre statue of Lionel Messi in Kolkata has been removed less than a year after its unveiling due to safety fears over its stability in strong winds.

stories.title: Pashinyan moves to defuse Russia tensions while pledging Armenia's sovereignty ahead of crucial vote
stories.summary: With the latest polls showing him on course for a landslide victory on Sunday, PM Nikol Pashinyan told Armenians – and Russia – that he is seeking a balanced yet sovereign approach to his foreign policy, as Russia continued to exert pressure by imposing more sanctions ahead of the crucial vote.

stories.title: Cryptocurrency addiction is real — and it looks just like gambling
stories.summary: Jamie Giles is a therapist at Castle Craig, one of Scotland's leading addiction treatment centres. He spoke to Euronews about how to spot problematic crypto investing, who is most at risk and what to do about it.

stories.title: China's highest bridge attracts millions of visitors
stories.summary: China's Huajiang Grand Canyon Bridge has cut travel times, expanded 5G access and helped boost tourism and business in rural Guizhou.

stories.title: Half of Belgium sees the US as a bigger adversary than China
stories.summary: According to a recent poll, over 50 percent of Belgians view the US as an adversary. Meanwhile, only 40% to 44% perceive China as a rival.

stories.title: Southern energy corridors set to dominate supplies to Europe, global leaders say
stories.summary: The rapid reshape of global energy supply routes and markets is a key focus at the Baku Energy Week 2026.

stories.title: IRGC says Iran has more 'trump cards' up its sleeve as talks hang by a thread
stories.summary: A senior IRGC general said Iran had not revealed all its military capabilities and was ready for direct confrontation with the US and NATO, as hardliners linked to acting IRGC commander Ahmad Vahidi were identified as the force behind Monday's suspension of indirect negotiations with Washington.

stories.title: Massive drug tunnel uncovered beneath US-Mexico border
stories.summary: US authorities released footage of a sophisticated tunnel running beneath the US-Mexico border, saying it was used to smuggle more than $45 million worth of cocaine from Tijuana into California.

stories.title: French artist JR's giant 'cave' installation in Paris appears damaged
stories.summary: Strong winds and heavy rain damaged artist JR’s giant “La Caverne” installation covering Paris’ Pont Neuf bridge, forcing organisers to postpone its public opening scheduled for June 6.

stories.title: A man in Turkey became one of the richest people in the world without realising it
stories.summary: With a balance of around $21.8 billion in his account, Takalo ranks among the world's super rich, but he has been unable to access his money for nearly a month.

stories.title: French navy boards Russia-linked oil tanker in Atlantic – video
stories.summary: France has released footage that appears to show French naval commandos boarding a ship suspected of being part of Russia's 'shadow fleet' and subject to international sanctions. The French president, Emmanuel Macron, said the operation took place on the tanker, named the Tagor, with support of the UK and in strict compliance with the law of the sea. The French navy confirmed the interception occurred on Sunday Macron says French navy has boarded Russia-linked oil tanker in Atlantic Continue rea

stories.title: Refugees and migration: Is Europe closing its doors?
stories.summary: Migration researchers have called on European governments to abandon isolationist policies. But many politicians are not heeding their calls.

stories.title: People ‘panicking’ as Ghana passes sweeping law criminalising LGBTQ+ activity
stories.summary: Community groups say some fear they could lose homes, jobs and access to healthcare if the new law is ratified by President John Dramani Mahama Ghana’s LGBTQ community is living in fear after the country’s parliament approved a sweeping bill that criminalises the promotion of LGBTQ+ activities and identifying as lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender or queer, rights groups have warned. The legislation, which was passed on Friday, mandates prison sentences of three to 10 years. Continue reading...

stories.title: Explosion at Malta fireworks factory sends plumes of smoke into air – video
stories.summary: The explosion happened at the Ta' Lourdes factory on the north side of the Mediterranean island on Monday morning. Two men in fields near the factory at the time of the blast were admitted to hospital with minor injuries. Residents in the surrounding area reported shattered windows and buildings shaken by the force of the blast. It is not yet known what caused the explosion Continue reading...

stories.title: Ethiopia goes electric following gas car ban
stories.summary: Electric vehicles have taken off in Ethiopia. Key to the shift: a world-first ban on importing fossil fuel-powered vehicles.

stories.title: Watch: Glass doors break as fans scramble to see Pursuit of Jade star
stories.summary: Huge crowds formed outside a shopping centre in China to see actor Zhang Linghe.

stories.title: Watch: Explosion at fireworks factory in Malta
stories.summary: Two men were injured in the explosion and were taken to hospital with minor injuries.

stories.title: Seven killed in Iowa shooting, including gunman
stories.summary: A shooting across several locations in Muscatine, Iowa, has left seven people dead, including the gunman.

stories.title: Israel and Hezbollah exchange fire despite Trump announcing breakthrough agreement
stories.summary: Israel and Hezbollah exchange fire after Donald Trump announced a halt, casting doubt on ceasefire efforts.
